methods.description: Process for examining cuticles and measuring stomatal density and size on both fossil and extant pinnule cuticles.

steps.description: Cuticles were examined with an Olympus AX70 microscope, and cuticle photos were taken using the AnalySIS software.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture cuticle images using a microscope and imaging software

steps.description: Stomatal density was measured by drawing a box measuring 400 # 400 mm on the cuticle photo and counting the number of stomata per box.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stomatal density by counting stomata in a defined area on cuticle images

steps.description: Stomatal size was calculated as the product of the length and width of between two and six guard cell pairs per piece of cuticle.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate stomatal size from guard cell dimensions

methods.description: Method for determining the area of pinnules using scanned images and image analysis software.

steps.description: Whole-pinnule images of herbarium, field-collected, and fossil specimens were captured using a CanoScan Toolbox flatbed scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: Scan pinnule specimens to capture images

steps.description: Pinnule area was determined with ImageJ software.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pinnule area using ImageJ software

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including tests for interactions between variables affecting pinnule anatomy or size.

steps.description: Shapiro-Wilk tests and multiple linear models with analysis of variance run on modern data used RStudio.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Shapiro-Wilk tests and ANOVA using RStudio

steps.description: Test for interactions between variables that had significant effects on pinnule anatomy or size.
steps.searchable_summary: Test for variable interactions affecting pinnule characteristics
